title: system tests: friendier messages for 2-arg is()
body:
The 'is' check was intended to be called with three arguments,
the last one being a nice helpful test name. There's a fallback
for two-argument calls, but it was a horrible FIXME.

New fallback: the most recently run podman command. We keep
track of it in each run_podman() invocation.

This is not ideal, because it's theoretically possible to
invoke 'is' on something other than the output of run_podman,
but this at least fixes the by-far-most-common case.

Signed-off-by: Ed Santiago <santiago@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *edsantiago* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [edsantiago] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

This brings podman tests more in line with buildah ones (which I wrote later, after having learned lessons from the podman ones).

Tested via:
```bash
@test "two-argument is() after run_podman" {
    run_podman ps -a
    is "$output" "these are not the droids you're looking for"
}

@test "two-argument is() with no previous run_podman" {
    is "a" "b"
}
```
Result:
```
 ✗ two-argument is() after run_podman
   (from function `is' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 417,
    in test file test/system/001-basic.bats, line 15)
     `is "$output" "these are not the droids you're looking for"' failed
   $ /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/bin/podman ps -a
   CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: podman ps -a
   #| expected: 'these are not the droids you're looking for'
   #|   actual: 'CONTAINER ID  IMAGE   COMMAND  CREATED  STATUS  PORTS   NAMES'
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   # [teardown]
   $ /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/bin/podman pod rm --all --force
   $ /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/bin/podman rm --all --force
 ✗ two-argument is() with no previous run_podman
   (from function `is' in file test/system/helpers.bash, line 417,
    in test file test/system/001-basic.bats, line 19)
     `is "a" "b"' failed
   #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
   #|     FAIL: [no test name given]
   #| expected: 'b'
   #|   actual: 'a'
   #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
   # [teardown]
   $ /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/bin/podman pod rm --all --force
   $ /home/esm/src/atomic/2018-02.podman/libpod/bin/podman rm --all --force
```
